pi_items.source: Task description line 1 and setup_task.sh confirms this path
Task description line 5: 'Export the random points layer as GeoJSON to: /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sampling_points.geojson'
Task description line 6: 'Export the attribute table as CSV to: /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sampling_coordinates.csv'
Task description: 5 points per polygon × 3 polygons (Austria, Switzerland, Czechia) = 15 total
Task description uses 'Random Points Inside Polygons' tool which generates point geometries
Web search confirms Switzerland min longitude is 5.7349, Austria is 9.48, Czechia is 12.09. Claimed 5.0 correctly encompasses all three countries. Verified against GIS Coordinates database.
Web search confirms Switzerland max longitude is 10.6677, Austria is 16.98, Czechia is 18.87. Claimed 19.0 correctly encompasses all three countries.
Web search confirms Switzerland min latitude is 45.6755, Austria is 46.43, Czechia is 48.55. Claimed 45.0 correctly encompasses all three countries.
Web search confirms Austria max latitude is 49.04, Switzerland is 47.9163, Czechia is 51.06. Claimed 52.0 correctly encompasses all three countries.
setup_task.sh downloads from Natural Earth. URL tested and confirmed accessible (HTTP 200, 214976 bytes)
Confirmed by reading Natural Earth shapefile: ADMIN field contains exact country names. setup_task.sh filters for ADMIN IN ('Austria', 'Switzerland', 'Czechia', 'Czech Republic')

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real geographic data from the Natural Earth 110m Countries dataset containing three Central European countries: Austria, Switzerland, and Czechia. The input file is generated by filtering the Natural Earth shapefile (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip) for these three countries. Users must generate 15 random points (5 per country) using QGIS's Random Points Inside Polygons tool and export results as GeoJSON and CSV files. The coordinate bounds (lon: 5.0-19.0, lat: 45.0-52.0) correctly encompass all three countries' geographic extents based on verified bounding boxes.
